children.text: to appoint and fix the compensation of such personnel as may be necessary to carry out this chapter, without regard to the provisions of title 5 governing appointments in the competitive service, but at General Schedule pay rates not in excess of the maximum rate for grade GS–15 of the General Schedule under section 5332 of that title;1See References in Text note below.1

to procure temporary and intermittent services of such experts and consultants as are necessary to the extent authorized by , but at rates not to exceed the rate specified at the time of such service for grade GS–18 in section 5332 of such title;section 3109 of title 5
to prescribe such regulations as it deems necessary governing the manner in which its functions shall be carried out;

to receive money and other property donated, bequeathed, or devised, without condition or restriction other than it be used for the purposes of the Foundation; and to use, sell, or otherwise dispose of such property for the purpose of carrying out its functions;
to accept and utilize the services of voluntary and noncompensated personnel and reimburse them for travel expenses, including per diem, as authorized by ;section 5703 of title 5

to enter into contracts, grants, or other arrangements, or modifications thereof, to carry out the provisions of this chapter, and such contracts or modifications thereof may, with the concurrence of two-thirds of the members of the Board, be entered into without performance or other bonds, and without regard to ;1section 6101 of title 41
to make advances, progress, and other payments which the Board deems necessary under this chapter without regard to the provisions of section 3324(a) and (b) of title 31;
to rent office space in the District of Columbia or its environs;
to conduct programs in addition to or in conjunction with the Fellowship program which shall further the Foundation’s purpose of encouraging research and study of constitutionalism in America;
to expend not more than 5 percent of its annual operating budget to pay the costs of fundraising activities, including public and private gatherings; and

children.heading: Annual report
children.label: b Annual report
children.text: The foundation shall submit to the President and to the Congress an annual report of its operations under this chapter.2So in original. Probably should be capitalized.2

children.heading: Detailing of agency personnel to Foundation
children.label: c Detailing of agency personnel to Foundation
children.text: On request of the Chairman of the Foundation, the head of a Federal agency may detail personnel of the agency to the Foundation to assist the Foundation in carrying out this chapter. Details under this subsection shall be without reimbursement by the Foundation to the agency from which personnel are detailed.1
